1	# Result dependency and rerun map
     2	
     3	**Instruction snapshot:** 2026-07-16, reviewed against the current working
     4	layout. This is a routing document, not evidence that a job ran or a result
     5	passed. Verified numbers live only in `../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`; live work in
     6	`OPEN_ITEMS.md`; bug state in `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`; and chronology in the
     7	dimensional RUN_LOGs.
     8	
     9	The July-16 presentation workstream and dated
    10	`jul-16-presentation/PRESENTATION_SAFE_TABLE.md` are complete. That closeout
    11	does **not** close publication items #15 or #16. Neither item requires a
    12	central-value rerun, and neither is recorded here as complete. The later
    13	full-event FPS PET finding (#19) is a separate estimator-definition change: it
    14	requires a new gated PET nominal and UQ campaign while preserving the old
    15	recoil-only nominal as a cross-check.
    16	
    17	## Status vocabulary
    18	
    19	| Mark | Meaning |
    20	|---|---|
    21	| **FROZEN-CV** | The central estimator and its validation record are frozen. No open audit item requires a central rerun. |
    22	| **REUSE** | The named input is valid for the next descendant and must not be regenerated. |
    23	| **REBUILD** | A covariance or other descendant must be regenerated before publication use. |
    24	| **QUARANTINE** | Preserve for provenance, but do not quote or feed into a publication descendant. |
    25	| **GATED** | Work may begin only after the named prerequisite and namespace checks pass. |
    26	
    27	“Frozen” applies only to the stated node. It never promotes an old covariance
    28	or a derived significance merely because its central value is frozen.
    29	
    30	## Publication dependency graph
    31	
    32	```mermaid
    33	flowchart TD
    34	  CV[Committed CV event loops and central estimators] --> C2[2D central]
    35	  CV --> C3[3D central]
    36	  CV --> C4[4D central]
    37	  CV --> C5[5D scalar central]
    38	  CV --> CF[FPS central]
    39	
    40	  C5 --> S5[Corrected 5D vertical/stat/ML/joint-throw trunk]
    41	  AU[Active-universe interface validation] --> AS[Standard active event loops]
    42	  AU --> AF[FPS active event loops]
    43	  AS --> L5[Selection-complete standard lateral block]
    44	  AF --> LF[Selection-complete FPS lateral block]
    45	  S5 --> A5[Publication 5D scalar covariance]
    46	  L5 --> A5
    47	  A5 --> P2[2D covariance projection]
    48	  A5 --> P3[3D covariance projection]
    49	  A5 --> P4[4D covariance projection]
    50	  A5 --> EW[(Eavail,W) covariance]
    51	  EW --> SIG[Covariance-dependent significances]
    52	
    53	  C4 --> U4[Corrected 4D UQ contract]
    54	  L5 --> U4
    55	  CF --> UF[Corrected FPS UQ contract]
    56	  LF --> UF
    57	
    58	  RPC[Legacy recoil-PET nominal] --> RST20[Legacy replicas 1-20]
    59	  RST20 --> RST100[Optional/in-flight replicas 21-100]
    60	  RST100 --> RX[Recoil-only cross-check]
    61	
    62	  CF --> FEI[Full-event FPS interface and CV gate]
    63	  FEI --> FEN[New full-event FPS PET nominal]
    64	  FEN --> FEU[Fresh PET stat/ML/vertical UQ]
    65	  AF --> FPC[Selection-complete shifted FPS clouds]
    66	  FPC --> FLAT[Full-event FPS lateral retraining]
    67	  FEU --> FETOT[Publication full-event FPS PET total]
    68	  FLAT --> FETOT
    69	  FETOT --> PET4[PET marginals, FPS tiers and comparisons]
    70	  RX --> PET4
    71	```
    72	
    73	## Frozen central results and current rerun frontiers
    74	
    75	| Family | Frozen/reusable nodes | Publication rerun frontier | Descendants that remain stale until the frontier lands |
    76	|---|---|---|---|
    77	| 2D | Phase-18.2 central value, paper comparison, dedicated validated 2D controls | No central work. Use the final 5D projection for the shared publication covariance; keep the dedicated 2D covariance as a disclosed cross-check. | Any lower-dimensional claim explicitly defined as a marginal of the final 5D covariance. |
    78	| 3D | Central value, dimensional anchor, and closure controls | No central work. Start at covariance projection from the final 5D trunk. | Projected 3D covariance and any covariance-dependent comparison. |
    79	| 4D scalar | Central value, dimensional anchor, existing coherent statistical replicas, fixed-seed ML replicas, universe inputs/banks that pass the corrected schema | Rebuild the 4D covariance under the corrected asymmetric-endpoint, fixed-estimator-seed, throw-mean-centered contract; incorporate the selection-complete standard lateral replacement or publish the exact 5D-to-4D marginal. | Old 4D adopted covariance and every number derived from it. |
    80	| 5D scalar | Central value; completed background-aware sweep; corrected vertical/stat/ML/joint-throw components | Start at #16 standard active-universe production, then replace the support-limited lateral block and re-adopt. Do not repeat the background-aware sweep. | Final adopted 5D covariance, 2D/3D/4D projections, `(Eavail,W)`, and significances. |
    81	| FPS scalar | Central value, anchors, closure/coverage controls, existing generator predictions, and reusable corrected-schema banks/replicas | Start with corrected FPS UQ assembly plus #16 FPS active-universe production. | Old FPS adopted covariance, FPS precision claims, and covariance-dependent FPS descendants. |
    82	| Legacy recoil PET | Corrected background-subtracted recoil input, nominal/floor, replicas 1-20, PET ML, vertical, current detector, and targeted vertical retraining components | #15 may add replicas 21-100 as a cross-check. If already running, let unique jobs drain; do not treat them as inputs to the new estimator. | A labeled recoil-representation comparison only; no component transfers automatically to full-event FPS PET. |
    83	| Full-event FPS PET | FPS CV event-loop provenance and the `xps2` gate/extended-edge convention are reusable as scaffolding; existing `xps2` clouds remain recoil-only | #19 begins at the paired full-event interface and FPS CV validation, then a new nominal/floor. P3F supplies selection-complete shifted endpoints; P3S is regression-only. | New nominal and all estimator-specific UQ, marginals, FPS tier comparisons and prior-envelope descendants. |
    84	| Generators | Existing generator production and qualitative shape/localization studies | Projection and significance code only, after the governing covariance is final. | Numerical chi-square/significance values. |
    85	
    86	## Scalar chain
    87	
    88	```text
    89	FROZEN-CV 4D/5D central values
    90	  + REUSE corrected background-aware 5D vertical sweep
    91	  + REUSE coherent scalar statistical and fixed-seed ML replicas
    92	  + REUSE corrected joint-throw slabs/banks where their manifests pass
    93	  + REBUILD standard active-universe endpoints (#16)
    94	    -> REBUILD selection-complete lateral covariance
    95	    -> REBUILD/adopt final 5D scalar covariance
    96	    -> REBUILD 2D/3D/4D and (Eavail,W) projections
    97	    -> REBUILD covariance-dependent significances
    98	```
    99	
   100	The independent 4D estimator may be published only after its own corrected
   101	contract is rebuilt. Otherwise, call the published 4D object the exact marginal
   102	of the final 5D estimator and keep the independent 4D result as a cross-check.
   103	
   104	## PET chains
   105	
   106	```text
   107	LEGACY RECOIL CROSS-CHECK:
   108	REUSE corrected bkg-subtracted recoil-PET input + nominal + floor
   109	  + REUSE replicas 1-20
   110	  + OPTIONAL/IN-FLIGHT replicas 21-100 (#15)
   111	  -> retain as a recoil-only comparison, not the publication estimator
   112	
   113	PUBLICATION FULL-EVENT FPS PET:
   114	REUSE FPS CV ROOT provenance + xps2 gate/extended-edge convention only
   115	  -> BUILD paired event_reco/event_data/event_truth + cloud interface
   116	  -> VALIDATE FPS denominator/misses/alignment + leakage and stress closures
   117	  -> BUILD new full-event FPS PET nominal + floor
   118	  -> BUILD fresh coherent stat, ML, vertical and retraining components
   119	  + REBUILD selection-complete shifted full-event inputs from #16 P3F
   120	    -> REBUILD full-event FPS lateral response/retraining
   121	  -> REBUILD total covariance, exact marginals, FPS tier comparisons
   122	  -> REBUILD anchor, extension closure/coverage and prior envelope
   123	```
   124	
   125	#15 changes the statistical component only. #16 changes support-dependent
   126	lateral components only. **Neither, by itself, invalidates the legacy
   127	recoil-PET nominal or requires a central-value rerun.** #19 independently
   128	defines a new full-event FPS estimator and therefore does require a new gated
   129	nominal; this is not a rerun or relabeling of the legacy estimator.
   130	
   131	## FPS chain
   132	
   133	```text
   134	FROZEN-CV FPS central + anchors + generator/prior inputs
   135	  + REUSE valid statistical/ML replicas and corrected-schema banks
   136	  + REBUILD corrected asymmetric/fixed-seed/mean-centered joint-throw result
   137	  + REBUILD FPS active-universe endpoints (#16)
   138	    -> REBUILD FPS lateral block
   139	    -> REBUILD/adopt corrected FPS covariance
   140	    -> REBUILD covariance-dependent FPS comparisons only
   141	```
   142	
   143	Standard and FPS active event loops are separate productions because their
   144	phase-space selections differ. A standard output cannot be relabeled as FPS,
   145	and an FPS output cannot silently replace a standard input.
   146	
   147	## Trigger-to-staleness lookup
   148	
   149	| Trigger | Earliest allowed restart | Exactly what becomes stale | Explicitly remains reusable |
   150	|---|---|---|---|
   151	| #15 adds legacy recoil-PET replicas 21-100 | Recoil-PET replica training/extraction for IDs 21-100 | Only the legacy recoil `C_stat` and cross-check total containing it | Legacy input/nominal/floor/IDs 1-20; every full-event FPS PET product |
   152	| #16 active interface changes before production | Interface smoke and metadata validation | No scientific result yet; discard only smoke artifacts made with the old interface | Every frozen central and completed background-aware result |
   153	| #16 standard active endpoints land | Standard lateral merge/unfold stage | Standard scalar lateral block; adopted 5D covariance; its projections and significances | CV central, vertical sweep, scalar stat/ML, completed background-aware sweep |
   154	| #16 FPS active endpoints land | FPS lateral merge/unfold stage | FPS lateral block, corrected FPS covariance, FPS covariance descendants | FPS central, prior/generator production, unrelated standard active outputs |
   155	| #19 full-event interface/feature contract changes | Full-event FPS CV dump and interface tests | New full-event nominal/floor and every new estimator-specific UQ descendant | All scalar results; legacy recoil-PET products as labeled cross-checks |
   156	| P3F selection-complete full-event FPS clouds land | Full-event FPS PET shifted-cloud processing | New PET lateral block, lateral retraining response, total/marginals/tier comparisons | New full-event nominal/floor and non-lateral components once P5A/P5B inventories pass; all P3S products |
   157	| Corrected 4D/FPS joint-throw convention or inventory changes | Stored slabs if compatible; otherwise the affected throw/block stage | Respective adopted covariance and its descendants | Central event loops and central estimator; other dimensional families |
   158	| Covariance projection/bin-volume mapping changes | Projection validation | Only projected covariances and their significances | Upstream covariance and all training/event loops |
   159	| Documentation or product-summary omission | Provenance commit | Quote/citation status only | Scientific artifacts; do not rerun them to repair documentation |
   160	
   161	## Hard no-rerun boundary
   162	
   163	Publication agents must not repeat:
   164	
   165	- valid 2D, 3D, 4D, 5D and scalar FPS central event loops/training, or legacy
   166	  recoil-PET central training. #19 authorizes a separately named full-event FPS
   167	  PET estimator only; never overwrite or relabel the legacy nominal;
   168	- the completed 5D background-aware 188-entry sweep;
   169	- PET statistical replicas 1-20;
   170	- completed PET vertical targeted-retraining studies;
   171	- generator production or generator event samples;
   172	- any presentation-only production or deck regeneration already marked
   173	  complete, unless the publication document packet finds a reproducible build
   174	  failure.
   175	
   176	The packetized execution contract is in
   177	[the publication completion runbook](PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md).
   178	Reorganization begins only after the publication-results tag described in
   179	[the post-publication reorganization plan](POST_PUBLICATION_REORG_PLAN.md).

1	# Publication completion runbook
     2	
     3	**Instructions only — not a run receipt.** This document assigns remaining
     4	publication work under the current repository layout. It does not show that any
     5	packet, job, validation, or commit has occurred. Completion state remains in
     6	`OPEN_ITEMS.md` and the dimensional STATUS files; verified results belong in
     7	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`; chronology belongs in the RUN_LOGs.
     8	
     9	The July-16 presentation is closed. Its dated Q&A source is
    10	`jul-16-presentation/PRESENTATION_SAFE_TABLE.md`; do not turn its preliminary
    11	labels into publication claims. Publication work must follow the dependency
    12	frontiers in `RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md`.
    13	
    14	## Global execution contract
    15	
    16	Every executing agent must do all of the following before launching, resuming,
    17	or combining work:
    18	
    19	1. Read `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `../2d-unfolding/2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md`,
    20	   `../nd-unfolding/ND_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`, and the applicable production
    21	   tracker. PET work also reads
    22	   `../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md` and
    23	   `../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_PRODUCTION_STATUS.md`.
    24	2. Run `git status --short`, inspect overlapping diffs, identify the owning
    25	   session for every dirty path, and record the source commit. Never fold an
    26	   unrelated dirty change into a packet commit.
    27	3. Check `squeue -u "$USER"`, relevant `sacct` receipts, and
    28	   `./alloc_run.sh --status`. Check both job identity and output paths. Never
    29	   submit a duplicate of queued/running work, cancel a job owned by another
    30	   session, race a writer, or call `alloc_run.sh --end` unless the user asks to
    31	   release the allocation.
    32	4. Enumerate the packet's output namespace and reject any nonempty target that
    33	   is not either a validated skip-if-exists input or an explicitly versioned
    34	   staging directory. Existing files are not permission to overwrite them.
    35	5. Use the installed binary
    36	   `MINERvA101/opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold`, per-playlist manifests, and the
    37	   environment from `setup_salloc_env.sh`. Never use a build-tree binary or a
    38	   combined MEFHC manifest.
    39	6. Keep production under the existing directory layout until the
    40	   publication-results freeze tag. No packet may reorganize files while it
    41	   executes.
    42	
    43	The same commit that makes a production result available must contain its
    44	launcher/code, exact tracked JSON or text product summary, a ledger entry, an
    45	append-only RUN_LOG entry, and a short STATUS update. Heavy ignored artifacts
    46	must be traceable from that summary by path, source commit, configuration,
    47	manifest, job receipts, size, and checksum or stable ROOT-content fingerprint.
    48	An uncommitted result is not a dependency for the next packet.
    49	
    50	## Global do-not-run boundary
    51	
    52	No packet is authorized to repeat:
    53	
    54	- any validated central 2D, 3D, 4D, 5D, or scalar FPS event loop/unfold, or the
    55	  legacy recoil-only PET nominal. The new full-event FPS PET nominal required
    56	  by KNOWN_ISSUES #19 is a distinct gated estimator, not permission to overwrite
    57	  or relabel the legacy nominal;
    58	- the completed background-aware 5D sweep (vertical, detector, and matched-CV
    59	  inventory);
    60	- PET coherent statistical replicas 1-20;
    61	- completed PET vertical targeted-retraining endpoints;
    62	- generator production or generator samples;
    63	- completed presentation production, deck rendering, or presentation-safe
    64	  table generation.
    65	
    66	Preserve those products as inputs or provenance. A missing summary is repaired
    67	with documentation, not by rerunning physics.
    68	
    69	## Packet P0 — provenance and worktree reconciliation
    70	
    71	**Purpose:** establish a clean, attributable starting point without changing a
    72	scientific result.
    73	
    74	- **Prerequisites:** none beyond the global contract.
    75	- **Permitted inputs:** `git status`, `git diff`, `git log`, current STATUS and
    76	  production trackers, existing compact product summaries, scheduler receipts,
    77	  and read-only artifact metadata.
    78	- **Protected paths:** every heavy result, canonical central product, active job
    79	  output, other-session diff, and append-only RUN_LOG history. Do not stage or
    80	  edit a file whose ownership is unresolved.
    81	- **Expected outputs:** an ownership inventory grouped into (a) already
    82	  committed, (b) complete result awaiting its gate commit, (c) in-progress
    83	  work, (d) transient job file, and (e) unrelated user change; scoped commits
    84	  that reconcile already-complete campaigns; no physics rerun.
    85	- **Validation gates:** each staged file has an owner and purpose; tracked
    86	  summaries point to extant artifacts; doc facts are in their canonical homes;
    87	  `git diff --check` passes; no job/output timestamp changed because of this
    88	  packet.
    89	- **Commit requirement:** commit only one coherent campaign at a time. If a
    90	  complete campaign lacks ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS entries, add them in the same
    91	  reconciliation commit. Do not claim incomplete #15, #16, 4D/FPS UQ, or
    92	  dependent significances.
    93	- **Downstream consumers:** every other packet. P1 and P2 may start only after
    94	  their own input paths are reconciled; they need not wait for unrelated dirty
    95	  families.
    96	
    97	## Packet P1 — #15 legacy recoil-PET replicas 21–100
    98	
    99	**Purpose:** enlarge only the corrected recoil-PET statistical inventory as a
   100	representation cross-check. Completion does not close the full-event FPS PET
   101	gate in KNOWN_ISSUES #19. If this packet is already queued or running, let its
   102	unique jobs drain; do not cancel, duplicate, or silently redirect their output.
   103	If it has not started, do not start it solely as a dependency of the new
   104	publication estimator.
   105	
   106	- **Prerequisites:** P0 has established ownership of the PET code and replica
   107	  namespace; corrected PET input, nominal, floor, and IDs 1-20 pass read-only
   108	  checks; no PET replica job is queued or running for IDs 21-100.
   109	- **Permitted inputs:** `of_inputs_pc_fullcloud_bkgsub_5d.npz`,
   110	  `of_inputs_5d.npz`, the corrected nominal/floor products under
   111	  `products/pet/bkgsub/`, replicas 1-20 under
   112	  `products/pet/bkgsub/bootstrap_replicas/`, and the committed
   113	  `pet/sbatch_pet_bootstrap_replica.sh` →
   114	  `pet/extract_bootstrap_replica.py` path. Set `PET_INPUTS` and
   115	  `PET_BOOT_OUTDIR` explicitly to the corrected paths; never accept the
   116	  launcher's unsubtracted default.
   117	- **Protected paths:** replica IDs 1-20; unsubtracted point-cloud input and old
   118	  bootstrap directories; corrected nominal/floor; current PET component and
   119	  total files; all GBDT UQ paths.
   120	- **Expected outputs:** weight, 4D diagnostic, and 5D cross-section NPZs for
   121	  exactly IDs 21-100 in the existing corrected replica namespace; then a staged
   122	  `products/pet/bkgsub/pet_cstat_bkgsub_5d_100rep.npz` and matching summary.
   123	- **Validation gates:** exact ID set 1-100 with no duplicate, gap, wrong shape,
   124	  partial file, or non-finite value; `mc_indices` are complete and ordered;
   125	  bootstrap seed metadata matches the filename; the coherent MC draw is used
   126	  in training and extraction; estimator seed and nominal configuration are
   127	  fixed; covariance is replica-mean-centered; common nominal mask/order is
   128	  exact; PSD/symmetry/finite-diagonal and floor comparisons pass.
   129	- **Commit requirement:** scripts if changed, exact 100-ID manifest, compact
   130	  `C_stat` summary, ledger, PET/ND RUN_LOG, and ND STATUS in one commit. Preserve
   131	  the 20-replica product as a named cross-check; do not overwrite it in place.
   132	- **Downstream consumers:** P5A recoil-versus-full-event validation and a
   133	  clearly labeled legacy cross-check only. P5B must create a fresh statistical
   134	  ensemble for the full-event FPS estimator. No scalar covariance consumes
   135	  these replicas.
   136	
   137	## Packet P2 — #16 active-universe interface validation
   138	
   139	**Purpose:** prove the promoted-universe event-loop interface before any
   140	120-task production family is launched.
   141	
   142	- **Prerequisites:** P0 assigns ownership of
   143	  `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp`, the installed binary, active-universe launcher,
   144	  and remediation tests. No production active-universe namespace is writable
   145	  by another session.
   146	- **Permitted inputs:** one small 1A smoke subset derived from the canonical 1A
   147	  manifests; `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE=BAND:IDX`; the five kinematic bands
   148	  `BeamAngleX`, `BeamAngleY`, `MuonResolution`, `Muon_Energy_MINERvA`, and
   149	  `Muon_Energy_MINOS`; `nd-unfolding/tests/test_uq_remediation.py`.
   150	- **Protected paths:** canonical 1A/MEFHC ROOT files,
   151	  `active_universe_5d/{standard,fps}/`, production manifests, and the installed
   152	  binary until its source/build provenance is recorded. Smokes go only under
   153	  `nd-unfolding/active_universe_5d/interface_smoke/`.
   154	- **Expected outputs:** a disposable CV-versus-one-endpoint smoke pair and a
   155	  tracked interface-validation summary; committed interface code/tests. Do not
   156	  retain smoke ROOTs as production inputs.
   157	- **Validation gates:** invalid band/index requests fail closed; active metadata
   158	  names the requested band/index and lateral status; truth and reco selection
   159	  are rebuilt; background follows the active universe; truth-authoritative IDs,
   160	  bilateral dedupe, and native misses remain valid; signal/truth finite-support
   161	  closure passes; truth/reco entrant and exit counters are finite and internally
   162	  consistent; point-cloud branches are complete when
   163	  `MNV101_DUMP_POINTCLOUD=1`; standard and FPS flags produce distinct metadata;
   164	  unit tests, compile, install, and smoke readback pass.
   165	- **Commit requirement:** source, tests, interface summary, RUN_LOG entry, STATUS
   166	  one-liner, and any ledger validation entry land together. Production launcher
   167	  changes may be held for P3 so the launcher and its product summary satisfy the
   168	  campaign commit gate together.
   169	- **Downstream consumers:** P3 only. A smoke pass is not evidence that any
   170	  active-universe production endpoint exists.
   171	
   172	## Packet P3 — standard and FPS active event loops
   173	
   174	**Purpose:** produce selection-complete endpoint inputs once, in disjoint
   175	standard and FPS namespaces, for both scalar and PET descendants.
   176	
   177	This packet may be assigned as two independent subpackets after P2:
   178	
   179	| Subpacket | Required flags | Output root |
   180	|---|---|---|
   181	| P3S standard | `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE=BAND:IDX`, `MNV101_DUMP_POINTCLOUD=1`, no full-phase-space flag | `nd-unfolding/active_universe_5d/standard/` |
   182	| P3F FPS | same active and point-cloud flags plus `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1` | `nd-unfolding/active_universe_5d/fps/` |
   183	
   184	- **Prerequisites:** P2 committed; scheduler and namespace preflight passes;
   185	  installed binary fingerprint matches P2; all five bands × two endpoints × 12
   186	  playlists are enumerated before submission.
   187	- **Permitted inputs:** canonical per-playlist Data/MC manifests and baseline
   188	  flux inputs. Use `sbatch_evloop_array_5d_active_laterals.sh` only after its
   189	  flags, account/QOS, output naming, and skip-if-exists behavior match this
   190	  contract.

1	# PET UQ remediation — dependency map
     2	
     3	**Scope:** PET point-cloud central value and uncertainty budget only. This file
     4	defines ordering and completion gates; job ownership and live execution remain
     5	in session-specific state files. Verified numbers belong in
     6	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md` only after their commit lands.
     7	
     8	## Bottom line
     9	
    10	Do **not** begin with full per-universe PET retraining. Every such training
    11	depends on the corrected background-subtracted measured target and nominal PET
    12	configuration. First build and validate that target, retrain the nominal model,
    13	and measure the ordinary statistical/ML spreads. Then use a small targeted set
    14	of systematic retrains to determine whether the frozen-reweighter approximation
    15	is adequate. Expand to more universes only if that response is material.
    16	
    17	The existing unsubtracted PET input, nominal weights, eight bootstrap replicas,
    18	and dependent covariances are cross-checks only. None may enter the corrected
    19	budget.
    20	
    21	## Dimensionality decision (gate 0)
    22	
    23	One point-cloud input cannot exactly reproduce both existing scalar purity
    24	targets: the 4D and 5D `measured_weights` differ on 3,836,331 of 4,091,707 data
    25	events (maximum absolute difference 0.838).
    26	
    27	Recommended production definition:
    28	
    29	- use `of_inputs_5d.npz` measured weights as the canonical 5D PET target;
    30	- build one coherent corrected 5D PET estimator and budget;
    31	- obtain 4D as an exact covariance marginal/projection of that 5D result and
    32	  label it **the 4D marginal of the 5D PET estimator**.
    33	
    34	Alternative (roughly doubles GPU work): train separate 4D- and 5D-target PET
    35	ensembles. This is required only if PET must match each separately trained
    36	scalar target rather than present one coherent 5D measurement and its marginal.
    37	Record the choice before launching the ensemble.
    38	
    39	## Ordered DAG
    40	
    41	### 1. Corrected measured target (CPU/input work)
    42	
    43	1. Preserve `of_inputs_pc_fullcloud.npz` unchanged as the unsubtracted control.
    44	2. Create a separately named background-subtracted input. Reuse the existing
    45	   complete cloud tensors/source omnifile; no new C++ event loop is needed.
    46	3. Store or reconstruct the data scalar coordinates and prove event-by-event
    47	   row alignment against the chosen scalar NPZ before copying its purity
    48	   weights. Equal row counts alone are not an alignment proof.
    49	4. Require finite weights in `[0,1]`; reproduce the scalar target sum and
    50	   per-bin `max(0, data-background)/data` factors exactly.
    51	5. Add a small complete end-to-end fixture that reaches PET extraction under
    52	   the ROOT environment.
    53	
    54	**Gate:** row alignment exact; target histogram and total weight exact; old
    55	input untouched; input provenance stored in a JSON/txt summary.
    56	
    57	### 2. Corrected nominal PET (GPU)
    58	
    59	Train one unbootstrapped nominal PET model on the corrected input with the final
    60	training-event count, iterations, epochs, estimator seed, and subsample seed.
    61	Extract its 5D cross section and chosen projections. All later blocks must embed
    62	this same reported-bin mask/order and corrected nominal central vector.
    63	
    64	**Gate:** finite full-coverage weights, strict ordered MC indices, extraction
    65	passes, dimensional marginal/normalization checks pass.
    66	
    67	### 3. Corrected GPU floor (GPU)
    68	
    69	Repeat the exact nominal configuration once. This measures nondeterminism for
    70	the corrected target; the old unsubtracted duplicate is not transferable.
    71	
    72	**Gate:** floor recorded before interpreting either `C_stat` or `C_ML`.
    73	
    74	### 4. Independent statistical and ML ensembles (GPU; may run in parallel)
    75	
    76	- **`C_stat`:** vary only the independent data/MC Poisson replica ID. Hold the
    77	  estimator and training-subsample seeds fixed. Apply the identical coherent MC
    78	  draw in training and final counts/completeness. Center covariance on the
    79	  replica mean and require an exact manifest.
    80	- **PET `C_ML`:** no Poisson fluctuation. Use the corrected input and vary PET
    81	  training randomness. Prefer a small crossed design over subsample/split seed
    82	  and TensorFlow estimator seed so their interaction is visible. The GBDT
    83	  `C_ML` scan does not substitute for this PET ensemble.
    84	
    85	Run pilots first; scale only after both spreads exceed the corrected GPU floor
    86	by an interpretable margin and the replica extraction gate passes.
    87	
    88	### 5. Rebuild frozen-reweighter systematic blocks (CPU/large-memory)
    89	
    90	Using the corrected nominal PET push weights:
    91	
    92	1. rebuild the vertical `C_syst` with actual minus/plus endpoints, exact 100-ID
    93	   flux inventory, MAT mean-centered biased `1/N`, and the agreed invalid-ratio
    94	   policy;
    95	2. rebuild the PET-native lateral block on selection-complete universe inputs;
    96	3. rerun the corrected PET unified/block diagnostic with individual-factor
    97	   clipping but no composite re-clip.
    98	
    99	The clean truth-ratio bank may support a **preliminary vertical** block because
   100	the frozen PET calculation consumes truth ratios only. A publication-final
   101	claim must still respect `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` #13/#16: use the new background-aware
   102	dump/re-bank and selection-complete lateral products owned by the shared GBDT
   103	campaign when available; do not independently duplicate that event-loop work.
   104	
   105	### 6. Targeted per-universe retraining-response study (GPU decision gate)
   106	
   107	Only now retrain a small, predeclared set of dominant/kinematic systematic
   108	endpoints. Include representatives of:
   109	
   110	- the dominant vertical model band(s);
   111	- flux (leading/effective modes rather than all 100 replicas for the pilot);
   112	- the dominant muon-energy/beam lateral endpoint(s), using genuinely shifted
   113	  clouds, selection, and background target.
   114	
   115	For each endpoint compare the fully retrained displacement with the matched
   116	frozen-reweighter displacement, after subtracting/bounding the GPU floor. Set
   117	the materiality criterion before looking at results (trace change plus
   118	per-bin-tail criterion).
   119	
   120	Decision:
   121	
   122	- **Compatible within the declared tolerance:** retain the frozen-reweighter
   123	  block, quote the targeted response study as its validation/bound, and disclose
   124	  that the full universe set was not retrained.
   125	- **Material for particular bands:** retrain those bands or construct an
   126	  explicitly documented response correction/envelope; repeat the gate.
   127	- **Broadly material:** only then consider a full per-universe campaign. Full
   128	  187-universe PET retraining is not the default and requires an explicit GPU
   129	  cost/benefit decision.
   130	
   131	### 7. Final assembly and commit gate
   132	
   133	Assemble, on one matched corrected PET central and ordering,
   134	
   135	`C_total = C_syst + C_stat + C_ML + C_lateral`.
   136	
   137	Before calling it final, require:
   138	
   139	- exact manifests and common central/mask/order checks for every block;
   140	- covariance symmetry, PSD/eigen diagnostics, finite diagonal, and exact 5D to
   141	  4D projection checks;
   142	- corrected GPU-floor comparison and closure/extraction validation;
   143	- unified/block and targeted-retraining verdicts stated without silently
   144	  adopting an old inflation;
   145	- products summary plus ledger, RUN_LOG, and STATUS updates in the same commit.
   146	
   147	Until gate 7 lands, use “corrected PET component/pilot” rather than “final PET
   148	budget,” and retain the old eight replicas only as an unsubtracted cross-check.
   149	
   150	## Reuse / rerun matrix
   151	
   152	| Item | Disposition |
   153	|---|---|
   154	| Complete point-cloud source omnifile/cloud tensors | Reuse after row-alignment proof |
   155	| Flux, POT, nucleon constant, GBDT completeness anchors | Reuse; keep provenance fixed |
   156	| Scalar 5D background-subtracted measured weights | Reuse as canonical target (recommended) |
   157	| Existing unsubtracted PC NPZ and eight replicas | Preserve, label cross-check only |
   158	| Old PET nominal/alternate weights | Do not use in corrected blocks |
   159	| Corrected nominal and floor duplicate | Rerun |
   160	| PET `C_stat` and PET-specific `C_ML` | Rerun independently |
   161	| PET vertical, lateral, unified/block products | Rebuild with corrected nominal weights |
   162	| Selection-complete universes/new background-aware bank | Reuse shared GBDT campaign products when they land |
   163	| Full per-universe PET retraining | Defer pending targeted response gate |

    16	
    17	## Status vocabulary
    18	
    19	| Mark | Meaning |
    20	|---|---|
    21	| **FROZEN-CV** | The central estimator and its validation record are frozen. No open audit item requires a central rerun. |
    22	| **REUSE** | The named input is valid for the next descendant and must not be regenerated. |
    23	| **REBUILD** | A covariance or other descendant must be regenerated before publication use. |
    24	| **QUARANTINE** | Preserve for provenance, but do not quote or feed into a publication descendant. |
    25	| **GATED** | Work may begin only after the named prerequisite and namespace checks pass. |
    26	
    27	“Frozen” applies only to the stated node. It never promotes an old covariance
    28	or a derived significance merely because its central value is frozen.

    14	## Global execution contract
    15	
    16	Every executing agent must do all of the following before launching, resuming,
    17	or combining work:
    18	
    19	1. Read `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `../2d-unfolding/2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md`,
    20	   `../nd-unfolding/ND_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`, and the applicable production
    21	   tracker. PET work also reads
    22	   `../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md` and
    23	   `../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_PRODUCTION_STATUS.md`.
    24	2. Run `git status --short`, inspect overlapping diffs, identify the owning
    25	   session for every dirty path, and record the source commit. Never fold an
    26	   unrelated dirty change into a packet commit.
    27	3. Check `squeue -u "$USER"`, relevant `sacct` receipts, and
    28	   `./alloc_run.sh --status`. Check both job identity and output paths. Never
    29	   submit a duplicate of queued/running work, cancel a job owned by another
    30	   session, race a writer, or call `alloc_run.sh --end` unless the user asks to
    31	   release the allocation.
    32	4. Enumerate the packet's output namespace and reject any nonempty target that
    33	   is not either a validated skip-if-exists input or an explicitly versioned
    34	   staging directory. Existing files are not permission to overwrite them.
    35	5. Use the installed binary
    36	   `MINERvA101/opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold`, per-playlist manifests, and the
    37	   environment from `setup_salloc_env.sh`. Never use a build-tree binary or a
    38	   combined MEFHC manifest.
    39	6. Keep production under the existing directory layout until the
    40	   publication-results freeze tag. No packet may reorganize files while it
    41	   executes.
    42	
    43	The same commit that makes a production result available must contain its
    44	launcher/code, exact tracked JSON or text product summary, a ledger entry, an
    45	append-only RUN_LOG entry, and a short STATUS update. Heavy ignored artifacts
    46	must be traceable from that summary by path, source commit, configuration,
    47	manifest, job receipts, size, and checksum or stable ROOT-content fingerprint.
    48	An uncommitted result is not a dependency for the next packet.

    50	## Global do-not-run boundary
    51	
    52	No packet is authorized to repeat:
    53	
    54	- any validated central 2D, 3D, 4D, 5D, or scalar FPS event loop/unfold, or the
    55	  legacy recoil-only PET nominal. The new full-event FPS PET nominal required
    56	  by KNOWN_ISSUES #19 is a distinct gated estimator, not permission to overwrite
    57	  or relabel the legacy nominal;
    58	- the completed background-aware 5D sweep (vertical, detector, and matched-CV
    59	  inventory);
    60	- PET coherent statistical replicas 1-20;
    61	- completed PET vertical targeted-retraining endpoints;
    62	- generator production or generator samples;
    63	- completed presentation production, deck rendering, or presentation-safe
    64	  table generation.
    65

    38	## Resume-skip validates existence, not completeness (found 2026-07-17, 4D corrected uthrow)
    39	`[[ -s $OUT ]] && skip` resume guards (used across many sbatch launchers) treat any non-empty
    40	output as done. Producers that atomic-save per-unit into one file (e.g. `unified_throw_cov.py
    41	do_throws` per-throw saves) leave VALID-but-partial files when interrupted — the resume then
    42	permanently skips them. Bit us: comb4dCc 55971617 failed on 15/160 missing throws because slabs
    43	31,34-39 were partial leftovers of an interrupted multinode run; all 40 array tasks "COMPLETED".
    44	Caught only by the combine's `--expected-throws` manifest gate. Repair: partials moved to
    45	`uq_4d/corrected/uthrow_slabs_4d/partial_20260716_interrupted/`, regen 56025478 -> combine
    46	56025481 -> adopt 56025483. Fix pattern for future launchers: content-validated resume (open the
    47	file, check unit inventory) or write-to-temp + rename-on-complete. (BEN-023.)

1	# Full-event PET feature contract + P5A validation (KNOWN_ISSUES #19)
     2	
     3	Owner: Publication Agent B (PET). Draft frozen for the P5A interface milestone, 2026-07-16.
     4	This is the feature contract the production estimator (P5B) must satisfy. It records the
     5	exact schemas, units, normalization, masks, truncation policy, unavailable counterparts,
     6	required new branches, cost estimate, and the recoil-only products quarantined as cross-checks.
     7	
     8	Naming: **full-event PET over the declared extended-FPS fiducial phase space** (NOT
     9	"full phase space"). Two independent mandatory changes vs the recoil-only campaign:
    10	(1) full-event representation; (2) trained/reported over the extended FPS measurement.
    11	
    12	## Architecture (user directive 2026-07-16)
    13	PET trains UNBINNED on CONTINUOUS features. The extended (pT,p‖) EDGES are used ONLY for
    14	domain retention, reporting, covariance construction, and validation — never as classifier
    15	inputs or training bins. Guarded by `assert_extended_fps_edges` (fail closed on paper grid).
    16	
    17	## Estimator ID + configuration fingerprint (single contract for central + ALL covariance)
    18	**TWO DISTINCT estimator IDs (orchestrator 2026-07-17; never share one ID across schemas):**
    19	- `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` = the FULL-schema publication estimator (full muon object:
    20	  px,py,pz,E,φ,charge,MINOS + reco vertex + view/timing + residual summaries). Requires the C++
    21	  full-event dump + fresh full-schema P3F. This is the publication target.
    22	- `pet-reduced-fps-cross` = the REDUCED {pT,p‖}-muon estimator that P5A validated (stress
    23	  closure, smokes, pilot CLM-006). CROSS-CHECK ONLY — never a publication lateral/central source.
    24	Every P5B component (central, C_stat, C_ml, vertical/flux, laterals, total) MUST carry ONE of
    25	these fingerprints and never mix the two; recoil-only PET UQ is NEVER attached to either.
    26	- **inputs:** FPS CV full-event point-cloud npz built with `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1` + the
    27	  finalized full-event dump (scaffolding today = `of_inputs_pc_fps_xps2.npz`, recoil-only
    28	  tensors → must be regenerated for the real central; see blockers).
    29	- **features:** reco cloud (E,pos,z; KNN coord (pos,z)); truth cloud (E,px,py,pz,pdg,theta,phi;
    30	  KNN coord (theta,phi)); `event_reco`/`event_data` = continuous reco muon {pT,p‖} (+ full
    31	  px,py,pz,φ,E,charge,MINOS + reco vertex + residual summaries once branches land); `event_truth`
    32	  = continuous truth muon {pT,p‖}. Edges are reporting/covariance/validation only, never inputs.
    33	- **preprocessing:** cloud ÷1000 (MeV→GeV, mm→m), non-finite→0 (pad/mask sentinel); event
    34	  features z-normalized over pass_reco (reco/data) / pass_truth (truth), !pass rows zeroed.
    35	- **backend:** vendored `omnifold_nn` PET (multi-input Model, explicit `coord_idx`, FiLM event
    36	  conditioning) + MultiFold; niter 2, epochs 8, batch 1024, Adam lr 1e-4, train subsample 2M.
    37	- **seed policy:** estimator seed 42 FIXED for central + vertical/end-to-end universes + C_stat
    38	  (so C_stat varies only the coherent data+MC Poisson replica id); C_ml varies subsample/split
    39	  seed × TF estimator seed (predeclared crossed design), no Poisson.
    40	- **nominal product / phase space:** `products/pet/fullevent_fps/pet_fullevent_fps_nominal_*` (P5B);
    41	  extended-FPS canonical (pT,p‖) grid (this file's CANONICAL_* edges).
    42	- **fingerprint recipe (computed at P5B nominal build, stored in the product summary):**
    43	  `sha256(git_commit(net.py,omnifold.py,dataloader.py,fullevent_fps_dataloader.py) ||
    44	  feature_list || preprocessing || edges || seed_policy || input_npz_sha)`. Every covariance
    45	  component summary must carry the SAME fingerprint or it is rejected at assembly.
    46	
    47	## Measurement domain
    48	- Source only FPS CV event loops from `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1` (drops the 4 truth-muon
    49	  kinematic cuts, keeps tracker fiducial, grows the native-miss set). Pass `--full-phase-space`
    50	  wherever the truth gate is reconstructed; consumers without the flag must verify embedded
    51	  FPS provenance and fail closed.
    52	- Canonical extended reporting grid (EXACT; `fullevent_fps_dataloader.CANONICAL_*`):
    53	  - pT   (16 edges): 0,0.07,0.15,0.25,0.33,0.4,0.47,0.55,0.7,0.85,1.0,1.25,1.5,2.5,4.5,30.0
    54	  - p‖   (20 edges): 0,0.75,1.5,2,2.5,3,3.5,4,4.5,5,6,7,8,9,10,15,20,40,60,120
    55	- Two-tier reporting (FPS_PILOT.md): Tier-1 acceptance-supported (eff≳2%), Tier-2
    56	  prior-dominated dead cells (~28% of rate) carry a prior-dependence band. Muon features
    57	  cannot turn zero-efficiency cells into measured cells.
    58	- Scaffolding only (recoil-only tensors, NOT publication inputs): `of_inputs_pc_fps.npz`
    59	  (standard gate), `of_inputs_pc_fps_xps.npz` (angular cut only). `of_inputs_pc_fps_xps2.npz`
    60	  has the right gate+edges but recoil-only tensors → used for alignment/provenance scaffolding;
    61	  the representation is REBUILT.
    62	
    63	## Feature schemas (three explicit schemas; no manufactured counterparts)
    64	
    65	### reco cloud (step-1 detector) / measured (data) — SAME observable contract
    66	| feature | col | unit | normalization | notes |
    67	|---|---|---|---|---|
    68	| recoil token E | 0 | GeV | ÷1000 (MeV→GeV) | energy>0 = valid-token / pad mask |
    69	| recoil token pos | 1 | m | ÷1000 (mm→m) | transverse position in cluster's view |
    70	| recoil token z | 2 | m | ÷1000 | KNN coord |
    71	KNN neighborhood coords = **(pos, z) = cols (1,2)** (detector geometry), set via PET `coord_idx`.
    72	Padding: zero-pad/truncate to num_part=12 (loader top-N by energy).
    73	
    74	### truth cloud (step-2)
    75	| feature | col | unit | norm | notes |
    76	|---|---|---|---|---|
    77	| FS-hadron E,px,py,pz | 0–3 | GeV | ÷1000 | muon±13 & ν removed at source |
    78	| pdg | 4 | — | raw | **retained** (recoil-only loader dropped it); learned embedding = production refinement |
    79	| theta,phi | 5,6 | rad | raw | appended angular direction; **KNN coords = (theta,phi)=(5,6)** |
    80	
    81	### event_reco / event_data (continuous, SAME observable schema) — the distinguished muon
    82	- ADOPTED NOW (reduced): `[muon_pT, muon_p‖]` (reco_scalars / measured cols 0,1), z-normalized
    83	  with the RECO-MC statistic (data uses the reco norm → no truth statistic touches it).
    84	- REQUIRED for production (needs new C++ branches, getters exist — FULL_EVENT_INTERFACE_REQUEST.md):
    85	  muon `px,py,pz,E,phi`, `charge/qp`, `MINOS match/range/curvature quality` (step-1 detector
    86	  context, reco+data ONLY), reco vertex `x,y,z`, residual-energy summary tokens
    87	  (unclustered/detector-region), recoil `view ID` + `timing`.
    88	- Detector/MINOS features are step-1 ONLY. NEVER a truth counterpart.
    89	
    90	### event_truth (DISTINCT schema, own normalization)
    91	- ADOPTED NOW: `[truth_muon_pT, truth_muon_p‖]` (truth_scalars cols 0,1), z-normalized with
    92	  the TRUTH-MC statistic. NO MINOS/range/quality/vertex-detector counterparts; no sentinels.
    93	- Production may add truth event quantities (truth vertex into event_truth only, truth W).
    94	
    95	### masks / types
    96	- Padding mask: token energy (col 0) == 0. (An explicit boolean mask input is a production
    97	  option; the energy-mask convention is documented and tested.)
    98	- Muon is a distinguished EVENT feature (FiLM conditioning), so cloud tokens are all recoil;
    99	  a muon/recoil/view type embedding is only needed if muon becomes a cloud token (production
   100	  option). Detector view distinction awaits the `part_reco_view` branch.
   101	
   102	## REDUCTION JUSTIFICATION (explicit, per user directive)
   103	The P5A INTERFACE milestone is validated with the reduced distinguished-muon set
   104	`{pT, p‖}` — the dominant muon kinematics the recoil-only estimator is blind to. This is
   105	sufficient to (a) prove the paired continuous-feature interface works end-to-end and (b)
   106	close the omitted-muon stress test. It is **NOT** a publication-ready feature set: P5A is
   107	NOT declared "passed" on this reduced set. The full schema above is required before the
   108	production nominal (P5B), and needs the C++ full-event dump + FPS-CV regeneration (blocked
   109	on the active-universe C++ owner; branch request filed, not edited while P3S runs).
   110	
   111	## Data-semantics note — reco-muon SENTINELS for FPS misses (found in P5A FPS smoke)
   112	In the FPS scaffolding, `reco_scalars` muon (pT,p‖) = **-9999** for every non-pass_reco event
   113	(28.6M/49.2M misses have no reconstructed muon; pass_reco muon is clean, mean 0.734). The
   114	full-event adapter therefore normalizes reco features over pass_reco events ONLY (truth over
   115	pass_truth ONLY) and zeroes the undefined (!pass_reco) rows post-normalization (they are masked
   116	by pass_reco in the step-1 loss). The production full-event input build MUST preserve this
   117	handling; a naive normalization over all rows pollutes the muon scale (was mean -5732 before
   118	the fix). This is a genuine reco/data-vs-truth alignment subtlety the recoil-only estimator
   119	never exposed (it fed no scalars).
   120	
   121	## CLM-007 data-scalar source (fixed 2026-07-17, verified defect from the fe-fps campaign)
   122	The xps2 scaffolding pc npz has NO `measured_scalars`, so the data-side muon features must come
   123	from an explicit, row-aligned source — NEVER a silent fallback to MC `reco_scalars` (that
   124	indexes MC rows by data positions and injects -9999 sentinels into the step-1 data classifier).
   125	`build_fullevent_loaders` now FAILS CLOSED on a missing `measured_scalars` unless
   126	`data_scalars_npz` is given; for xps2 use `of_inputs_5d_fps_xps2.npz` (`measured` cols 0,1 =
   127	data muon pT,p‖; 4,116,128 rows == measured_pc, row-count gate enforced). Regression tests:
   128	`tests/test_fullevent_fps.py` CLM007DataScalarGuard (fail-closed + row-mismatch). The
   129	production full-event FPS-CV npz should carry `measured_scalars` directly (dump-time), removing
   130	the need for the sidecar. NOTE: row-count alignment is enforced; a full event-by-event order

131	proof (as `build_bkgsub_pointcloud_input.py` did against the ROOT) is a P5B hardening item.
   132

   135	- Muon full 4-vector/charge/vertex/view/timing at reco/data: pending C++ branches.

   182	From the fe-fps campaign V1 (codex) code audit. Each item is dispositioned; none blocks P5A.
   183	- **F10 truth-KNN φ not periodic — FIXED** (2026-07-17): `build_truth_cloud` encodes azimuth as

    48	### PET full-event + FPS measurement-domain gate (KNOWN_ISSUES #19)
    49	
    50	The present PET step-1/step-2 classifiers see only the reconstructed recoil
    51	cloud and truth-hadron cloud. Muon/scalar arrays are phase-space and extraction
    52	metadata, not classifier features. Binning a recoil-derived event weight in
    53	muon coordinates does not unfold the full joint distribution: at fixed recoil,
    54	the conditional muon distribution remains the generator prior. Therefore use
    55	**full-event** for the input representation and **extended phase space** for the
    56	relaxed acceptance; do not use “full phase space” as a synonym for a recoil
    57	point cloud. The publication deliverable requires both changes. A full-event
    58	classifier trained on the standard restricted phase space does not close this
    59	item.
    60	
    61	Mandatory measurement-domain contract:
    62	
    63	- source only FPS CV event loops produced with
    64	  `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1`, which remove the four truth muon kinematic cuts
    65	  while retaining the tracker fiducial definition and unchanged reconstructed
    66	  selection;
    67	- preserve the FPS truth denominator, newly admitted native misses, signal,
    68	  background, data, and event alignment through every cloud dump, scalar/W
    69	  source, train, extraction, systematic endpoint, projection, and closure;
    70	- pass `--full-phase-space` wherever code reconstructs the truth gate. A path
    71	  that consumes finalized inputs without that option must instead verify
    72	  embedded FPS provenance and reject incompatible metadata;
    73	- use the canonical extended FPS `(p_T,p_parallel)` edges, including the low-
    74	  and high-`p_parallel` and high-`p_T` catch bins. Record and compare the exact
    75	  arrays and reported-bin ordering; fail closed on the standard paper grid;
    76	- use P3F products under `active_universe_5d/fps/` for selection-complete
    77	  laterals. P3S products under `active_universe_5d/standard/` are regression
    78	  controls only and cannot be relabeled or reused as FPS endpoints;
    79	- report acceptance-supported and prior-dominated extrapolation regions
    80	  separately, and repeat the FPS anchor, extension closure, coverage, and
    81	  prior-swap/envelope controls for the new estimator.
    82	
    83	Artifact guard: `of_inputs_pc_fps.npz` applies the standard truth gate, and
    84	`of_inputs_pc_fps_xps.npz` lifts the angular cut but retains the standard
    85	`p_T/p_parallel` bounds. Neither is an FPS publication input. The `xps2`
    86	gate-and-edge convention is the semantic starting point, but its current
    87	recoil-only tensors are not the final full-event representation.
    88	
    89	Define three explicit event-feature schemas rather than manufacturing
    90	counterparts that do not exist:
    91	
    92	- `event_reco` and `event_data` use the same observable schema: a distinguished
    93	  reconstructed muon with full direction and momentum
    94	  (`p_x,p_y,p_z` or `p_T,p_parallel,phi`), energy and charge/type, plus MINOS
    95	  range/curvature and match-quality context only where its identical data/MC
    96	  definition is validated;
    97	- `event_truth` uses a distinct truth schema with the truth-muon four-vector and
    98	  truth event quantities. Never create truth MINOS/range/match counterparts or
    99	  fill detector-only features with sentinels;
   100	- the recoil set with energy and unambiguous geometry: view ID plus position and
   101	  `z`, preferably coordinates relative to the interaction vertex, with timing
   102	  and cluster/prong/type information when available;
   103	- truth-particle four-vectors plus a categorical PDG/type encoding (the PDG is
   104	  already dumped but the current loader discards it);
   105	- reconstructed vertex quantities may enter `event_reco/event_data`; truth
   106	  vertices may enter `event_truth`. A step-1 MC or data classifier must never
   107	  receive the MC truth vertex. Include explicit summaries for information
   108	  outside the retained cloud, including constituent count,
   109	  discarded/unclustered energy and detector-region energy totals, or use a
   110	  validated variable-length scheme that removes the fixed top-12 loss;
   111	- masks/type embeddings that distinguish muon, recoil constituents, padding,
   112	  detector views, and any residual-summary token.
   113	
   114	Do not feed generator interaction mode, generator-only process labels, or other
   115	unobservable truth labels into the publication classifier. Incoming-neutrino
   116	energy and similar truth-only latents require a separate prior-dependence case;
   117	they are not part of the default full-event claim. Run/playlist may be used only
   118	as validated detector-period conditioning, never as an unchecked data/MC label
   119	shortcut.

   307	    adopted 13.69%/bin rather than the block-sum 13.43%/bin, per item 11);
   308	    the PET 5D verdict (WORSE vs GBDT, indicative/2M-train-anchored, per
   309	    item 13's caveat).
   310	
   311	## Methodology stance (for the eventual response-to-referees)
   312	
   313	- Covariance is block-summed (C_syst+C_stat+C_ML); the unified-throw study
   314	  tests the linearity assumption directly and, in 4D, found it broken
   315	  (block-sum underestimates ~2×) — the published 4D systematic adopts the
   316	  unified-throw magnitude.
   317	- Central value: single-run CV; ensemble-mean CV agrees at 0.28%.
   318	- The corrected 4D/5D ML band isolates train/test-split response at fixed
   319	  estimator seed 42. Pure estimator-seed sensitivity is not added separately;
   320	  disclose this deliberate scope with any replacement budget.
   321	- GoF reported both binned (truncated-spectral χ²) and unbinned (C2ST).

    74	**Defining properties of the Phase-18.2 pipeline:**
    75	1. **Truth-tree-authoritative reco gate.** Event loop walks `mc_truth_denom`
    76	   first to build a `(mc_run, mc_subrun, mc_nthEvtInFile)` key set, then
    77	   walks reco and fills `mc_signal_reco` only when the key is in the set.
    78	   Truth-only events are appended as native OmniFold miss entries.
    79	2. **Bilateral upstream-dup dedupe.** Both loops dedupe on the same key
    80	   (handles 1,102 truth + 7 reco duplicates from one upstream-double-filled
    81	   AnaTuple, `MasterAnaDev_mc_AnaTuple_run00111353_Playlist.root`).
    82	3. **By-construction completeness.** `mc_signal_reco` entries ==
    83	   `mc_truth_denom` entries (32,849,103 each at MEFHC). The Phase-16 c
    84	   division in `unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned.py` becomes a no-op self-check.

    86	**Residual disagreement.** The remaining ~3 χ²/ndf is dominated by
    87	sub-2 % shape disagreement in the highest p_|| tails (paper / weighted
    88	reaches 1.15 at 40-60 GeV/c). Attributed to a small reweighter-detail
    89	effect at high E_ν. Method-blindness confirmed by IBU 1D-projection
    90	cross-check (post-Phase-16): IBU and OmniFold-2D agree on the same inputs
    91	to ~1.7 %, both reproduce paper to within ~1-2 %.

   431	
   432	## Running compute on interactive nodes (salloc) — hard-won lessons (2026-06-04)
   433	
   434	When the batch (shared/regular) queue is fairshare-throttled (you get only ~1-2 slots after
   435	running many jobs), `qos=interactive` is a SEPARATE queue that grabs a fresh node fast. It is
   436	worth using for **many small concurrent jobs that fit on one node** (e.g. lean npz-based
   437	bank-unfolds, or the ~12 per-playlist event loops). It is NOT a substitute for a large array
   438	(can't fit hundreds at once; interactive is time-/alloc-limited, typically <=2 allocs, <=4h).
   439	
   440	Pitfalls that cost real time here — DO NOT repeat:
   441	
   442	1. **Run the orchestrator INSIDE the salloc.** `salloc --qos interactive ... bash orchestrator.sh`
   443	   where the script launches up to ~10 concurrent `srun --overlap --exact -n1 -cN ...` steps,
   444	   then `wait`. Working examples: `nd-unfolding/run_q3_sweep_interactive.sh`,
   445	   `run_pc_evloop_interactive.sh`. Do NOT salloc-then-external-`srun --jobid=<id> --overlap`
   446	   from another shell: it races node configuration and fails with
   447	   "Unable to create step ... Invalid job id specified".
   448	
   449	2. **MONITOR BY OUTPUT ARTIFACTS, NOT salloc stdout.** salloc/srun stdout is heavily BUFFERED
   450	   when backgrounded/non-tty, so a perfectly healthy run shows an EMPTY log for many minutes.
   451	   A working q3 sweep was once CANCELLED because the log looked "stuck" — it was actually
   452	   mid-processing (visible only via `pgrep -af srun` and the produced `.root` files). Track
   453	   progress by counting output files + `squeue -j <allocid>` liveness; give each job its full
   454	   runtime before judging. Use skip-if-exists so cancel/relaunch is always safe & resumable.
   455	
   456	3. **Clean up holders.** `alloc_run.sh --end` scancels the SLURM job but the login-node
   457	   `salloc ... sleep` CLIENT process can linger and accumulate. After --end, verify with
   458	   `squeue --me --name=claude-hold` AND `pgrep -u $USER -af 'qos interactive'`; scancel/kill
   459	   leftovers so the queue stays clean.
   460	
   461	4. **Never background salloc with a bare `&`** from a returning shell (SIGHUP risk). Use the
   462	   harness `run_in_background`, or `nohup`/`setsid`.
   463	
   464	5. The `alloc_run.sh` wrapper (one held node, reused across calls, auto-renews past 3h) is the
   465	   right tool for SERIAL on-demand validation/plotting (login node can't run ROOT/LGBM).
   466	   For PARALLEL throughput, use the inside-salloc orchestrator pattern above instead.

1	# bkg_negweight — progress log (claude-school session)
     2	
     3	Study: unbinned background subtraction via negative-weight injection, the
     4	continuous analogue of the current per-reco-bin purity down-weight. Brief:
     5	`HANDOFF.md` beside this file. Advisor picked negweight; it becomes default
     6	ONLY if it validates AND the user signs off. Do not flip the default or re-run
     7	any headline product.
     8	
     9	Convention: newest entry at the bottom of the log; the memo + toy live in the
    10	two sections below.
    11	
    12	---
    13	
    14	## Phase 1a — Estimator memo (what the signed-weight step-1 classifier targets)
    15	
    16	### Setup (matches the real pipeline)
    17	OmniFold step-1 is a binary classifier (`unbinned_unfolding/python/omnifold.py`
    18	lines 246–256). Each iteration it pools two labelled samples on the reco
    19	manifold and fits with per-event `sample_weight`:
    20	
    21	- class 0 = MC signal reco (`MCreco`), weight `weights_push · MCreco_weights`
    22	  — call its weighted density **S(x)** (the current pushed simulation density);
    23	- class 1 = "measured", weight `measured_weights` — weighted density **ρ₁(x)**.
    24	
    25	It then reweights MC by `r(x) = p(x)/(1−p(x))`, where `p` is the classifier's
    26	class-1 probability (`reweight`, omnifold.py:76–83).
    27	
    28	### Population optimum of the weighted classifier
    29	The weighted binary cross-entropy at a point x is minimized pointwise by
    30	
    31	    p*(x) = ρ₁(x) / ( ρ₀(x) + ρ₁(x) ),      ρ₀ ≡ S,
    32	
    33	so the learned reweight converges to the density ratio
    34	
    35	    r*(x) = p*/(1−p*) = ρ₁(x) / S(x).
    36	
    37	This is the standard OmniFold likelihood-ratio trick; nothing here is new yet.
    38	The whole question is **what ρ₁ is** under each background treatment.
    39	
    40	### Current binned purity method  →  ρ₁ = max(0, D − B)
    41	Data events carry the per-reco-bin scalar
    42	`w_pur(b) = max(0, N_data(b) − N_bkg(b)) / N_data(b)`
    43	(`unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned.py:build_measured_training_2d`, ~L306). With data
    44	density D(x) and POT-scaled background density B(x), the weighted measured
    45	density is
    46	
    47	    ρ₁(x) = D(x) · w_pur(x) = D(x) · max(0, D−B)/D = max(0, D(x) − B(x)).
    48	
    49	So the *estimand already is* the background-subtracted data density D−B; the
    50	binning is only the mechanism, and `max(0,·)` floors the (rare) bins where the
    51	MC background over-fills the data.
    52	
    53	### Proposed negative-weight injection  →  ρ₁ = D − B (unbinned)
    54	Append the background-MC reco events to the **measured/class-1** side with
    55	weight `−w_bkg · pot_scale`; data events keep `+1`. The class-1 weighted density
    56	is then the literal difference
    57	
    58	    ρ₁(x) = D(x) − B(x)      (continuous, no analysis binning),
    59	
    60	because the +1 data events integrate to D and the negatively-weighted bkg-MC
    61	events integrate to −B. Hence
    62	
    63	    r*(x) = (D(x) − B(x)) / S(x).
    64	
    65	**Conclusion.** Purity and negweight target the *same estimand* r* = (D−B)/S.
    66	They differ only in realization: purity bins the data and down-weights each data
    67	event by a per-bin scalar (analysis binning enters here — the one place it does
    68	before final histogramming); negweight subtracts the background density
    69	event-by-event in the full reco phase space, binning-free. Negweight is exactly
    70	the unbinned version of the current correction, which is what the advisor asked
    71	for.
    72	
    73	### The `max(0,·)` floor in the unbinned world
    74	Where B(x) > D(x) locally, the true ρ₁ = D−B is negative. Two facts:
    75	
    76	1. A probabilistic classifier's output p∈(0,1) forces r = p/(1−p) ≥ 0, so the
    77	   learned reweight **cannot** be negative — it is *structurally* clipped at 0.
    78	   That matches the spirit of `max(0,·)`, but for the wrong reason: in a B>D
    79	   region the class-1 weighted mass is negative, the pointwise loss
    80	   `−ρ₁ log p − ρ₀ log(1−p)` is no longer convex with a minimum in (0,1), and
    81	   p* need not lie in [0,1]. The fit is ill-posed there, not gracefully floored.
    82	2. So the unbinned analogue of the floor is a *training-stability* question, not
    83	   just a post-hoc clip. Options, in increasing robustness:
    84	   - (a) **post-hoc clip** the learned reweight at 0 (cosmetically matches
    85	     `max(0,·)`; does nothing for the corrupted fit that produced it);
    86	   - (b) **Stay Positive** (arXiv:2505.03724): refine the signed-weight measured
    87	     sample into an equivalent *positive*-weight sample before training, so the
    88	     classifier only ever sees non-negative weights and the loss stays
    89	     well-posed. This is the principled fix and becomes `--bkg-mode
    90	     negweight-refined` (implemented later, as a separate mode).
    91	
    92	The toy below measures how badly (1) actually bites for a GBDT with negative
    93	sample weights, and whether a simple post-hoc clip suffices at our ~3%
    94	background level or whether Stay Positive is needed.
    95	
    96	---
    97	
    98	## Phase 1b — LightGBM signed-weight toy
    99	
   100	Script: `negweight_toy.py` (pure numpy + lightgbm, runs on the login node in
   101	~1 min with `OMP_NUM_THREADS=4`, no ROOT/SLURM). Saved output:
   102	`negweight_toy_output.log`. 1D ground truth with closed-form target:
   103	signal MC ~N(0,1), data signal ~N(0.3,1.1), background ~N(2.0,0.7), 16.7%
   104	contamination, N_Smc=N_Dsig=40k so the analytic target is
   105	`r*(x) = (D−B)/S = φ(x;0.3,1.1)/φ(x;0,1)` — a nontrivial smooth positive ratio.
   106	
   107	### Results (median | 90th-pctile abs rel-err vs the analytic target, signal core |x|<3)
   108	| mode | target it should hit | med rel-err | p90 | note |
   109	|------|----------------------|-------------|-----|------|
   110	| none (no subtraction) | D/S | 0.063 | 0.147 | but **0.109 biased** vs the clean (D−B)/S — contamination is real |
   111	| purity (binned max(0,(D−B)/D)) | (D−B)/S | 0.067 | 0.159 | current method |
   112	| **negweight** (matched bkg, 3 seeds) | (D−B)/S | **0.068 ±0.003** | 0.221 | proposed method |
   113	| purity vs negweight agreement | 1 | 0.089 | 0.286 | same estimand, differences at GBDT-noise scale |
   114	
   115	**Findings.**
   116	1. **Estimator confirmed.** Negative-weight injection recovers the analytic
   117	   (D−B)/S at the same accuracy as the binned purity method (med ~6.8% vs ~6.7%,
   118	   all GBDT sampling noise, not method bias), and the two agree with each other
   119	   at that same noise scale — empirical confirmation of the memo's claim that
   120	   they target one estimand. Skipping subtraction leaves a clear ~11% bias, so
   121	   the correction matters.
   122	2. **Seed-stable.** Over 3 LightGBM seeds the negweight rel-err is 0.068 ±0.003
   123	   — the negative sample weights do not destabilize the fit.
   124	3. **LightGBM tolerates negative sample weights.** No NaN/inf in any learned
   125	   reweight, no crash — even in the over-subtraction stress. So Stay-Positive is
   126	   a *refinement*, not a prerequisite for the method to run.
   127	
   128	### Over-subtraction stress (genuine local B>D)
   129	Tail background ~N(3.8,0.5) (sits in the sparse signal tail) with 1.3× injected
   130	background → 20% of the grid has true (D−1.3B)/S < 0.
   131	- Over that true-negative region the learned reweight max = **1e-6** — i.e. the
   132	  probabilistic classifier's p→0 there, so `r=p/(1−p)→0`. The reweight is
   133	  **structurally floored at 0**, exactly reproducing the current `max(0,·)`; a
   134	  post-hoc clip at 0 is exact.
   135	- BUT the fit in a B>D region is not well-posed (the class-1 weighted mass is
   136	  negative there), so the structural floor is a lucky artifact of the [0,1]
   137	  probability range, not a principled estimate. **That region is precisely where
   138	  `negweight-refined` (Stay Positive, arXiv:2505.03724) earns its keep** —
   139	  refining the signed sample to a positive-weight equivalent before training so
   140	  the fit is well-posed there instead of merely clipped.
   141	
   142	### Verdict / go-signal for Phase 2
   143	Negweight validates in the toy: correct estimand, GBDT-noise-level accuracy,
   144	seed-stable, LightGBM-safe. Implement `--bkg-mode {purity,negweight,
   145	negweight-refined}` in the 2D driver (default `purity`, headline byte-identical
   146	when unset). For the initial `negweight` mode, floor the learned reweight at 0
   147	(post-hoc clip) as the documented unbinned analogue of `max(0,·)`;
   148	`negweight-refined` (Stay Positive) is a later, separate mode for the B>D
   149	regions. **Stop here and report to the user before touching the driver** (per
   150	HANDOFF plan, stop between phases 1 and 2).
   151	
   152	### Sanity checks performed on this result
   153	- Analytic target is closed-form (Gaussian ratio), not itself estimated, so the
   154	  rel-err numbers are absolute, not relative to another fit.
   155	- First stress attempt (background under the signal core, μ_B=2.0) produced an
   156	  EMPTY true-negative region (frac=0.00 → nan): 1.3× over-subtraction there
   157	  never drove D−B<0 because the signal bulk dominates. Fixed by moving the
   158	  stress background into the signal tail (μ_B=3.8), which is also the physically
   159	  honest B>D scenario. The headline none/purity/negweight comparison keeps the
   160	  realistic overlapping background (μ_B=2.0).
   161	- Ops note: first two runs thrashed the shared login node (LightGBM grabbed all
   162	  cores; a grep-piped background run also left a stray full-core process). Rerun
   163	  with `OMP_NUM_THREADS=4` + `n_jobs=4` + unbuffered stdout; ~1 min clean.
   164

+
+## 2026-07-11 (claude-school) — FPS/ND-scale validation (Step 1 gate) STARTED
+User authorized making negweight+Stay-Positive the default for FPS/N-D/PET and
+regenerating the headline central + covariances under it. Step 1 = validate at
+FPS/ND scale (the gate). The FPS config runs through the ND driver
+(unfold_nd_omnifold_unbinned.py), NOT the 2D driver: `--axes ""`=2D,
+`eavail,q3`=4D, `eavail,q3,W`=5D, all with `--full-phase-space --pt-edges $PT_EXT
+--pz-edges $PZ_EXT` on runEventLoopOmniFold_5D_FPS_MEFHC.root. `--bkg-mode`
+default: ND line 499, 2D line 1008.
+
+### 1L smoke (FPS 1L omnifile, 1 iter, lgbm; purity vs negweight-refined) — PASS
+Runs clean at 2D/4D/5D + a 2D closure; no crashes. Per-bin CV agreement
+(negweight-refined/purity on hXSecND_flat, significant bins):
+  2D: nsig 211, sum-ratio 0.9979, median 1.0004, [0.932,1.039], 99% <5%
+  4D: nsig 2333, sum-ratio 0.9994, median 0.9982, [0.949,1.045], 100% <5%
+  5D: nsig 3686, sum-ratio 0.9967, median 0.9947, [0.901,1.034], 99% <5%
+Stay-Positive floors cleanly at zero where B>D: 2D 0 clipped, 4D 8/6751
+(g in [0.276,0.996]), 5D 11/6751 (g in [0.250,0.997]) -- clip frac rises with
+dimension (finer bins -> more local B>D), weights stay non-negative, no blow-up.
+Closure (2D refined, pseudo-data = sim reco + injected bkg contamination):
+hXSecND/hClosureRef median 0.9999, 100% within 5% -- clean truth recovery with
+the injected background subtracted. => negweight-refined ~= purity even at FPS
+(large background), so per the plan Step 3 collapses to re-anchor+relabel for the
+ND/GBDT path (not a from-scratch recompute).
+
+### MEFHC headline-stats confirmation LAUNCHED
+2D-FPS MEFHC matched pair (purity vs negweight-refined, 5 iter, lgbm, seed 1,
+the documented xsec_2d_FPS_MEFHC config) running in parallel in the shared
+alloc (55795538); logs $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/fps_mefhc/. Confirms full-stats
+agreement before the gate is declared closed.
+
+### SCOPE FINDINGS surfaced to user (block Steps 2-4 as originally framed)
+1. **PET-FPS central is un-background-subtracted (undocumented).** The PET
+   point-cloud pipeline does NOT use --bkg-mode and applies NO subtraction on
+   the measured side: dump_pointcloud_inputs.py:177 writes
+   measured_weights=np.ones; MC side is mc_signal_reco (signal-only,
+   runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp:864-865). PETxsec5D.xsec (pet_systematics_5d.py) has
+   no bkg/purity term. A mc_background tree exists but is never read by the PET
+   path. The 0.9884 closure uses signal-only MC-as-pseudo-data -> blind to
+   background. => "negweight default for PET" is NOT a flip; it needs bkg
+   injection ADDED to the point cloud + Stay-Positive + a FULL GPU retrain
+   (~9h) + envelope re-run, AND it would SHIFT the PET central DOWN by the
+   (large FPS) background fraction -- the re-anchor/relabel logic (which assumes
+   negweight~=purity) does not apply to PET.
+2. **ND 4D/5D covariances go through sweep_bank{,_5d}.py, which does NOT consume
+   --bkg-mode** (per the earlier ND note). Regenerating 4D/5D syst covariance
+   under negweight needs negweight wired into the banked sweep + a re-bank on
+   the new-binary omnifiles (gated, overlaps #13). The FPS-2D central + its
+   universe cov CAN be regenerated directly via the ND driver --universe on the
+   on-disk runEventLoopOmniFold_5D_FPS_MEFHC_universes_full.root (193GB).
+Awaiting user decision on PET scope + 4D/5D-cov scope before committing the
+expensive Step-3 regeneration.
+
+## 2026-07-11 (claude-school) — USER DECISIONS locked (scope for Steps 2-4)
+- PET: **Option A / FULL negweight-by-injection**. Blocked by a hard obstacle
+  discovered via rootls on runEventLoopOmniFold_PC_FPS_1L.root: the `mc_background`
+  tree has ONLY scalars (sim_background,_pz,_eavail,_q3,_W,_pass,w_bkg) and NO reco
+  point clouds (part_reco_E/pos/z live only on mc_signal_reco + data). So literal
+  negative-weight injection into the neural PET requires REGENERATING the FPS PC
+  omnifile with background clouds: C++ change to runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp
+  (add part_reco_* to mc_background, gated by MNV101_DUMP_POINTCLOUD) -> rebuild ->

+- Covariances: re-anchor+relabel + ONE FPS-2D universe/bootstrap spot-check via the

+- Scope: FPS-family + PET ONLY.

+file, check unit inventory) or write-to-temp + rename-on-complete. (BEN-023.)

+### PET full-event + FPS measurement-domain gate (KNOWN_ISSUES #19)
+
+The present PET step-1/step-2 classifiers see only the reconstructed recoil
+cloud and truth-hadron cloud. Muon/scalar arrays are phase-space and extraction
+metadata, not classifier features. Binning a recoil-derived event weight in
+muon coordinates does not unfold the full joint distribution: at fixed recoil,
+the conditional muon distribution remains the generator prior. Therefore use
+**full-event** for the input representation and **extended phase space** for the
+relaxed acceptance; do not use “full phase space” as a synonym for a recoil
+point cloud. The publication deliverable requires both changes. A full-event
+classifier trained on the standard restricted phase space does not close this
+item.

+  that consumes finalized inputs without that option must instead verify
