item.text:
# BLOCK

The documents can currently authorize purity-footed, reduced-schema, or incompletely assembled results as publication products.

## Publication-material defects

1. **Scalar FPS is incorrectly frozen/reusable on the purity footing.**

   Evidence: P3F specifies no `--bkg-mode`, P4 consumes its outputs, and P6 permits existing FPS central values, replicas, and banks ([runbook line 179](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:179), [line 217](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:217), [line 334](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:334)). The rerun map explicitly freezes the FPS central and reuses existing components ([map line 81](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:81), [line 134](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:134)). Current state says all ten existing endpoint unfolds are purity controls and cannot be adopted ([takeover line 15](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:15)); the hard gate requires explicit, mode-stamped `negweight-refined` production ([rollout line 98](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/ROLLOUT-PLAN.md:98)).

   Smallest correction: quarantine every purity-footed FPS central/component as a matched control; require explicit `--bkg-mode negweight-refined`, distinct mode-stamped atomic outputs, exact manifests, and fresh adoption for P3F-scalar/P4-FPS/P6-FPS. Remove permission for early P6-FPS work on existing purity inputs.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

2. **The publication PET measured target is not defined as literal background-cloud injection plus Stay-Positive.**

   Evidence: P5A allows generic FPS CV inputs and P5B requires only a “background-subtraction proof” and a non-negative target ([runbook line 261](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:261), [line 301](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:301)). The remediation document instead directs copying scalar purity weights ([PET remediation line 43](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:43)). The locked decision requires aligned background clouds injected at negative POT-scaled weight followed by Stay-Positive ([background decision line 767](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/2d-unfolding/HANDOFF_bkg_negweight/bkg_negweight_state.md:767)); the current rollout makes this a pre-nominal hard gate ([rollout line 81](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/ROLLOUT-PLAN.md:81)).

   Smallest correction: make `negweight-refined` an explicit estimator-fingerprint field; require aligned background clouds, scalars, keys, and `w_bkg`; define the signed data-plus-background target and Stay-Positive normalization/telemetry; retain purity only as a matched control.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

3. **F7 is underspecified and therefore does not guarantee a coherent bootstrap.**

   Evidence: P5B says only “coherent bootstrap draw through extraction,” while the PET remediation covers generic data/MC Poisson replicas without a background inventory or per-replica refinement ordering ([runbook line 310](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:310), [PET remediation line 76](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:76)). F7 requires full data, signal-MC, and background-MC draws before subsetting, background factors before Stay-Positive, persisted seeds/factors, and exact reuse wherever each inventory participates ([rollout line 89](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/ROLLOUT-PLAN.md:89), [takeover line 19](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:19)).

   Smallest correction: add F7 as an explicit prerequisite for every P5B `C_stat` launch, with exact three-inventory manifests, draw-before-subset ordering, per-replica refinement, persisted replay data, and training/extraction identity tests.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

4. **G2, fresh full-schema P3F, and estimator identity are missing from the ordering.**

   Evidence: P3 claims one P3F production can serve scalar and PET descendants; P5A may run while P3F runs; P5B accepts those P3F roots ([runbook line 174](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:174), [line 253](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:253), [line 294](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:294)). Current P3F is pre-full-event/reduced schema ([rollout line 33](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/ROLLOUT-PLAN.md:33)). The contract reserves `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` for the full schema and labels the reduced estimator a cross-check ([feature contract line 17](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:17)); G2 regeneration is mandatory ([line 138](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:138)). Current takeover ordering is G2, regenerated FPS CV, then fresh full-schema P3F ([takeover line 20](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:20)).

   Smallest correction: split scalar P3F from PET full-schema P3F; quarantine present reduced endpoints as `pet-reduced-fps-cross`; insert G2 and regenerated full-schema FPS CV before both publication P5A and fresh PET P3F; require the exact `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` fingerprint on central and every covariance component. Preserve the binary ownership/drain gate before G2.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

5. **The full-event PET systematic contract can still be interpreted as frozen-map plus retraining components.**

   Evidence: the map calls for fresh “vertical and retraining components,” while P5B uses the ambiguous phrase “vertical/retraining response” ([map line 118](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:118), [runbook line 304](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:304)). The remediation DAG expressly allows retaining the frozen reweighter after a targeted study ([PET remediation line 88](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:88), [line 120](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:120)). Publication P5B instead requires joint end-to-end physical variation plus retraining, with no additive `C_syst + C_retrain` construction ([feature contract line 223](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:223), [line 229](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:229)).

   Smallest correction: state the joint-universe formula and nuisance ownership explicitly in P5B and the dependency map; prohibit separate additive retraining covariance; require per-universe background refinement and the common estimator fingerprint.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

6. **`PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md` masquerades as the publication PET DAG even though it is the legacy recoil/standard-domain plan.**

   Evidence: it declares general PET central/UQ scope, chooses `of_inputs_5d.npz` and copied scalar purity weights, then instructs nominal, floor, ensemble, systematic, and lateral rebuilding ([line 3](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:3), [line 21](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:21), [line 29](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:29), [line 150](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:150)). This contradicts the runbook’s recoil-only quarantine and no-rerun boundary ([runbook line 50](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:50), [line 97](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:97)) and the full-event contract’s no-transfer rule ([feature contract line 159](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:159)).

   Smallest correction: relabel the existing DAG and reuse matrix explicitly as a non-executable legacy recoil-PET cross-check record. Add or point to a separate publication DAG for `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` containing G2, negweight-refined, F7, fresh P3F, and joint UQ. Remove commands that would repeat completed legacy products.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

7. **The 4D rerun frontier is stale.**

   Evidence: the map and P6 describe rebuilding the corrected 4D contract and its throw blocks ([map line 79](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:79), [runbook line 325](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:325)). The current handoff records the repaired R1 corrected-4D chain as committed and independently checked; only the selection-complete standard lateral swap remains ([handoff line 34](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-HANDOFF.md:34), [line 63](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-HANDOFF.md:63), [takeover line 17](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:17)).

   Smallest correction: freeze the committed R1 non-lateral components; make P6-4D a pure, hash-checked lateral-component replacement and re-adoption after validated P3S/P4-standard. Do not rerun throws or other corrected components.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

8. **No packet actually assembles and adopts the final 5D scalar covariance.**

   Evidence: P4 outputs a lateral block and merely says it “feeds” final 5D adoption ([runbook line 226](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:226), [line 242](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:242)); P6 covers only 4D and FPS ([line 325](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:325)); P7 then assumes final covariances exist ([line 364](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:364)).    Smallest correction: add an explicit final-5D adoption subpacket with component identities, non-mutation hashes, block-sum/reconstruction checks, covariance validation, summary/ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS commit, and a hard P7 dependency.

   **Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

9. **Canonical-home and durability rules are not satisfied.**

   Evidence: the PET remediation says verified numbers belong only in the ledger, then writes exact decision-driving numbers locally ([PET remediation line 6](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:6), [line 23](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:23)). The dependency map says live state is elsewhere but contains a frozen/current-state table that is already stale ([map line 3](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:3), [line 73](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:73)). Current takeover state explicitly records PG0 as blocked because these controls are untracked while their canonical indexes are dirty ([takeover line 45](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:45)).

   Smallest correction: move verified numbers to the ledger and replace them with pointers; keep current completion state solely in OPEN_ITEMS/STATUS; limit the map to dependencies/invalidation; then land the repaired controls together with, or after, scoped owned commits for their canonical indexes and locked-decision record.

   **Classification: canonical-home defects block committing the plan; unresolved PG0 ownership/durability blocks production only.**

## Independent post-repair checklist

- [ ] Every scalar FPS production input and output is explicitly `negweight-refined`; purity artifacts are control-only.
- [ ] PET uses literal aligned background-cloud injection, Stay-Positive, and a frozen `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` fingerprint.
- [ ] F7 covers full data/signal/background inventories before subsetting and is exactly replayable.
- [ ] G2 and regenerated full-schema FPS CV precede fresh full-schema P3F; reduced P3F is quarantined.
- [ ] PET vertical/flux covariance is joint end-to-end, with no separate additive retraining block.
- [ ] Legacy recoil remediation is labeled non-publication and authorizes no duplicate production.
- [ ] R1 4D components are reused; explicit final 4D and 5D adoption packets exist.
- [ ] All skips are content-validated or atomic, with mode/schema/config manifests.
- [ ] Canonical indexes, summaries, ledger, RUN_LOG, STATUS, and control documents are committed before any dependent production starts.
